source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Both transistors \( T_1 \) and \( T_2 \) shown in the <image> have a threshold voltage of 1 Volt. The device parameters \( K_1 \) and \( K_2 \) of \( T_1 \) and \( T_2 \) are, respectively, \( 36 \, \mu \text{A} / \text{V}^2 \) and \( 9 \, \mu \text{A} / \text{V}^2 \). The output voltage \( V_o \) is ( ) V
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image object placeholder, not an extractable path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure', so the circuit diagram is required to determine the topology
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is primarily visual rather than solvable from text alone
the task asks for output voltage of a transistor circuit; circuit connectivity and device placement are typically only available in the figure
asset_registry_record shows image exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual asset is available
multi_step: solving for V_o in a transistor circuit usually requires reading the circuit from the image and then applying device equations, implying at least image interpretation plus electrical calculation
question_text provides threshold voltages and device parameters for two transistors, suggesting combination of multiple conditions rather than a single lookup
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests upstream considers the item to have meaningful reasoning depth
score kept moderate rather than very high because the exact circuit structure is not described in text, so full step count cannot be confirmed from fields alone
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='3', giving a clear target output
the expected response is a single voltage value, which is straightforward to check against a derived solution
asset_registry_record.answer_manifest.answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking
circuit-analysis problems with fixed parameters are usually rule-checkable once the figure is available

decision_reasons: Question, image, and numeric answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
The sample has high image dependency and high verifiability, indicating a usable visual reasoning item with a checkable target answer.
Although there are moderate risks about image readability and exact reasoning depth, the core semantic assets are available, so rejection or demotion is not warranted.
